summary: Test suite is incomplete and violates core requirements. aggregate.bats tests the script directly despite explicit requirement to test only through act pipeline. workflow.bats references fixture files (run1.xml, run2.json) and workflow YAML that are not provided/shown. Test 1 assumes pre-existing fixtures while Test 2 creates them inline — inconsistent. Missing coverage: shellcheck validation, bash -n syntax checks (required by task), error conditions (malformed JSON/XML, missing jq), edge cases (special chars, boundary values, empty results), negative test cases (script failures, act failures). aggregate.bats has reasonable happy-path coverage but lacks rigor for error scenarios. workflow.bats structure is sound but depends on missing fixture files, making assertions unverifiable. PIPESTATUS handling in run_act_with_fixtures() after tee pipe is questionable and may not capture act's actual exit code.
